title: copisma caribaeum
intro: Copisma caribaeum, also known as the Caribbean copseya or Copseya caribaea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae. It is a shrub or small tree native to the Caribbean islands and Central America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of Copisma caribaeum, such as teas and extracts, have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and respiratory infections, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
